agent_file.note: OpenCode walks up from CWD looking for AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md. Global fallbacks in this order. Also supports additional files via opencode.json `instructions` array.

rules.native_name: No dedicated rules directory — injected via instructions array or AGENTS.md
search_paths: AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md discovery (walks up directory tree)
rules.note: OpenCode has no ~/.config/opencode/rules/ equivalent. Rules content must live in AGENTS.md or be listed in opencode.json `instructions` array with glob patterns like `.cursor/rules/*.md`.

skills.note: OpenCode natively reads ~/.claude/skills/ as a fallback. The existing Claude Code symlink at ~/.claude/skills/ -> primitives/skills/ is ALREADY sufficient. No separate opencode skills dir needed.

commands.note: Frontmatter supports: description, agent, model, subtask. Template vars: $ARGUMENTS, $1/$2, !`shell`. Filename becomes slash command name.

custom_tools.native_name: TypeScript/JS files in tools/ or via plugin system
custom_tools.note: Uses tool() helper from @opencode-ai/plugin. Filename becomes tool name. Can also be registered inside a plugin module.

hooks.note: Hooks are TypeScript functions inside plugin modules, not shell scripts. No standalone hooks config file.

mcp_servers.note: type: local (stdio) or remote (HTTP/SSE). Supports OAuth for remote servers. CLI: opencode mcp auth <server-name>
mcp_servers.verify: python3 -c "import json,os; d=json.load(open(os.path.expanduser('~/.config/opencode/opencode.json'))); exit(0 if d.get('mcp') else 1)"

subagents.native_name: agents/ directory (.md files) or opencode.json `agent` key
subagents.note: Frontmatter fields: mode (primary/subagent/all), model, description, steps, permission. Body is system prompt. Invoked via @agent-name syntax.

plugins.native_name: TypeScript/JS plugins in plugins/ directory or npm packages
plugins.note: Plugins are async functions receiving {project, client, $, directory, worktree}. Can export hooks + tools. Bun auto-installs npm plugin deps at startup.
